title: Ivy League vs Community College: Which Education Is Better? | Middle Ground

title: Ivy League vs Community College: Which Education Is Better? | Middle Ground
body: No one asked but I graduated highschool in 2020 with a 2.0 gpa (3.0 on a 5.0 scale) and a low SAT so naturally I had no choice but to choose a community college. I attended a community for the first 2 years, had a straight 4.0 for both years, transfered over to a private D1 university and had scholarships which brought my yearly tuition down from $55K to $20K. I've had a straight 4.0 since I've transferred over and I'm planning on going to law school after I graduate in 2024. So far, I was accepted into Yale, Columbia, Duke and UChicago. Would I be able to do all of this without my community college? Absolutely not. It's a wonderful stepping stone and I don't think anyone should be ashamed of it.
author: @Nidrizzle

title: Ivy League vs Community College: Which Education Is Better? | Middle Ground

title: Ivy League vs Community College: Which Education Is Better? | Middle Ground
body: The college education system in America is unfair because your mistakes follow you like a criminal record. I get academic dishonesty or plagiarism, but not getting a bad grade in a class. It makes going back to school extremely difficult, expensive, and can automatically disqualify you from so many opportunities just because of mistakes you made when you were a teenager.

title: Ivy League vs Community College: Which Education Is Better? | Middle Ground
body: I’m a community college student, and I can tell you no matter what college you go to life is not easy. That last question really hit me, and yes, you DO need to know someone to get somewhere you want to be. I’ve had different job interviews that wouldn’t give me a chance. The two jobs I did get, one seasonal and the one I’m working now, let me work for them because I knew someone in the company. Same at my college, my mom knows someone in the school counseling office and I was able to get the help I needed. And no matter what school you go too, you are NEVER guaranteed a job after school
author: @alexdelaossa2024

title: Columbia Student RANKS the Ivy Leagues...
body: To the high schoolers and young people that I imagine watch these videos types of videos, please do not put too much weight on a university’s ranking when making your decision. It’s nearly impossible to objectively rank a university and most of, if not all of the ranking methods are flawed in some way. In academia these rankings are NEVER considered and some of the best research is coming out of schools that a lot of students will write off due to their ranking.
author: @michaels8902

title: College Admissions 101: What Do Colleges Look For? | The Princeton Review
body: Trust me! I watch this video everyday! So that I stay motivated. I am trying my best to get into any IVY league college, I want to cry tears of joy when I get accepted into Princeton. Let's see what happens...
author: @emdadmahfuz645

title: College Admissions 101: What Do Colleges Look For? | The Princeton Review
body:
Well this might sound weird but I come from a place where we never heard of counselors, never heard of IB, AP, SAT etc etc. But still dared to dream of Princeton.
Everytime I go through the videos of people who made it to an Ivy, I cry thinking that I always thought I was no less than those who are privileged enough to have so much exposure in their life. For me, winning small debate competitions and making some money out of it was a matter of pride.
The school you study matters. Money to study in those schools matter

It breaks my heart that even though I have dared to dream, will I be able to fulfill it or cry the rest of my life.
Whoever is reading this, please pray for me..
I can't even ask money to my parents for filling up the application form thinking I might be a burden. But prayers work.. Do remember me in your prayers so that I can make my parents proud 🙃💙
author: @MehfuzaAhmed01

title: College Admissions 101: What Do Colleges Look For? | The Princeton Review
body:
You know the US College Admissions is quite rigorous but it depends on the School but surely the High School Transcript and the essay are crucial.
The SAT and other Test Scores are also important.
Another matter is to use your Intution about what School would be good and great for you out there

title: I Spent a Real Day With Princeton Students
body: 10:24 for those who are wondering, the topic is called complex analysis, more specifically the contour integral (i.e. integrating over a closed curve) of a monomial being 2πi only when the exponent is -1. This is useful as you can compute the integral of a function that is expressed as a sum of monomials z^k where k is an integer, you can ignore all terms but the k=-1 term because they all give integral 0.

author: @SunsetMaximus

title: Steve Jobs' 2005 Stanford Commencement Address
body: “Your time is limited, so don’t waste it living someone else’s life. Don’t be trapped by dogma which is living with the results of other people’s thinking. Don’t let the noise of others’ opinions drown out your own inner voice. And the most important, is to have the courage to follow your heart and intuition. They somehow already know what you truly want to become. Everything else is secondary.”
author: @feliciaangkl

title: Steve Jobs' 2005 Stanford Commencement Address
body: I regularly comes here every month or whenever I got confused about life, facing chaos, less motivated and tired of hustling, this speech gives me hope & reason to fight. This is not just a speech but a philosophy of life. Thank you for this steve.
author: @chetanpanwar5703

title: Steve Jobs' 2005 Stanford Commencement Address
body:
Today is my 33rd birthday.
Somehow through the years this speech came back in my mind. Again and again. I watched it quite often now - almost once a year...
Years passed by and I always tried to stay hungry. Tried to stay foolish.
Today I am a well payed engineer in Germany. Working for the management of a huge global company. I became a private pilot. I do marathons and ultras...
...and I stay hungry. Looking forward for new achievements. And I stay foolish. I just applied for a management job for our hospitals. Trying to go beyond...
This speech is truely inspiring. Thank you, Steve! Thank you..
author: @mikeg.614

title: *BRUTALLY REALISTIC* Harvard Finals Week Vlog
body: My purpose is to empower as many students as possible so I put together a mass document of ALL 13 of my FREE guides PLUS 5 NEW college admission courses so just DM me “MENTOR” to my Instagram @UltimateIvyLeagueGuide and I’ll send it to you ✅😊

title: *BRUTALLY REALISTIC* Harvard Finals Week Vlog
body: I’ve seen studying until the ice melts, studying until the candle dies out, but I’ve never seen studying until the paper runs out!! 😰🙈
author: @sophie_59306

title: *BRUTALLY REALISTIC* Harvard Finals Week Vlog
body:
I am an 18-year-old girl living in South Korea, and although I should be a high school student, I dropped out of school two years ago. I wasn't interested in studying (to be precise, I hated schoolwork—after dropping out, I taught myself Spanish and French, and now I can speak them fluently). Yes, I just didn't want to do it. For the past two years, I’ve been working part-time jobs to make money. I thought I’d probably just keep living like this until I die. That was before I watched your video three weeks ago.

I’m sure many people have told you this before, but: my life has changed. After watching your video, I started thinking about what my life would have been like if I had gone to college. I’ve wanted to be an astronomer for a long time. I thought about it for a week, and eventually quit my part-time job. I returned to my parents’ house. (I had been living alone because I dropped out of school and had a strained relationship with my parents.) My parents were surprised but listened quietly to my story. I reconciled with them and am now preparing to return to high school. I want to tell you that it’s all thanks to you. I’m truly grateful...😊🎉
author: @YanYanhome

title: *BRUTALLY REALISTIC* Harvard Finals Week Vlog
body: You did a full gym workout, ate healthy meals and did skincare with all that studying, girl I am impressed. Most people are like gym who? Skin what? Ramen and Red Bull baybee.

title: *BRUTALLY REALISTIC* Harvard Finals Week Vlog

title: *BRUTALLY REALISTIC* Harvard Finals Week Vlog
body: "Sleep deprived" is exactly how I would describe my university experience. I remember hating every second of my last year (I loved my practices but HATED everything related to my classes and thesis), but nowadays I look back at that time with so much admiration. I learned so much.

title: *BRUTALLY REALISTIC* Harvard Finals Week Vlog
body: Another finals week hack: record yourself speaking your notes then playback when on the treadmill. I would hit the gym twice a day because of all the sitting/studying. You're still studying, but also exercising and reducing stress. 🤞🏾
author: @Sonia-rn1zz

title: *BRUTALLY REALISTIC* Harvard Finals Week Vlog
body: I have to say, one of my biggest struggles when studying is trying to stay productive but then getting distracting, or even finding that my teacher has given me bonus work for midterms, and I don't donut, but everyone else does. So honestly, props to you.
author: @talia6965

title: *BRUTALLY REALISTIC* Harvard Finals Week Vlog
body:
This is why people like her go to Harvard. She woke up, recorded herself, made herself launch and dinner, ran to the store, went to the library to study, and then had time to make 2 video calls?! I lock myself in my room and only eat and take a shower.😂
Though, to be fair, and despite the humor, I appreciate this video, clearly pressuring myself is not doing me any favor. I tried this semester to manage my time better, and I will continue to do so until hopefully I can be like her!

title: *BRUTALLY REALISTIC* Harvard Finals Week Vlog
body: Sometimes I feel like I threw away a lot of opportunities when I applied for an average uni with the grades I had. But watching these videos reassures me that those famous unis are not the place for me. I’d probably give up and if I fought through it I’d feel like it was all for nothing. All I want is to have a comfortable life and I know that this uni will give me that, so why push myself to the breaking point yk? At the same time tho I always wonder how far I could have gone had I wanted to… Life decisions…

title: HARVARD PRE-MED *REALISTIC* DAY-IN-MY-LIFE
body:
As a US med student at a prestigious program, now halfway through my third year, I’ll just share a little unsolicited advice. Make sure to take time for yourself and enjoy your undergraduate experience. Kudos to you for putting in all this work in extracurriculars and resumé building; it’s clear that you’re highly motivated. I will say, though, that a career in medicine is a LONG road and you can only go 100mph for so long before burning out.

The field of medicine is full of neuroticism which applies constant needless pressure to be the best, but I promise you it will be fine and work out well in the end. Take time for yourself: get enough sleep, watch a new series, hang out with friends, visit family. Taking care of yourself in these ways is essential because, otherwise, you may become resentful or regret going into the field.

I also promise that there will be enough time for the essentials (GPA, MCAT prep, research, clinical experience, applications, etc), and you will be more successful in them if you’re healthy in mind and body. Sorry for the rant and I wish the best for you and your future career in our field!
author: @ToastyMuffinz

title: HARVARD PRE-MED *REALISTIC* DAY-IN-MY-LIFE

author: @milo__thefish

title: HARVARD PRE-MED *REALISTIC* DAY-IN-MY-LIFE
body:
It may be easier said than done, but please prioritize your overall health (physical, mental, and emotional). If you are not careful, the load of college and trying to be on top of things chains you. I remember during undergrad and graduate school, I developed gastritis because I was not eating well. I would only get 3 hours of sleep per night. My mental health sank. I had panic attacks to the point I thought I was experiencing a heart attack. I went to the clinic, and they had to send me to the hospital.

Something someone told me, “Do you think your college degree is going to help you when you are a patient in the hospital or six feet under? No. Try creating a balance”.

Please try taking care of yourself now, or else spend the remainder trying to fix it.
author: @donelmalahoo6683

title: HARVARD PRE-MED *REALISTIC* DAY-IN-MY-LIFE
body: This reminds me of my junior/senior years in high school. It was just go go go no breaks gotta keep up with homework, Extracurriculars, work, internship, personal projects, etc. It was absolutely draining. I’m halfway through the first year of college and I no longer stress myself out like that. It’s not worth it. I can still achieve success leading a slower life

title: HARVARD PRE-MED *REALISTIC* DAY-IN-MY-LIFE
body: I am a final year med student who used to work just like that. The chronic stress gave me a brutal adrenal insufficiency diagnosis. So watch out to your sleep and overall health
author: @SaraKho

title: HARVARD PRE-MED *REALISTIC* DAY-IN-MY-LIFE
body: I‘m a German med studeng and I’m so happy to not have to live like this. I work hard as well, but I keep an eye on sleeping enough and I always keep my sundays study free. Wouldn’t want to risk my health and well being for a prestigious uni😢
author: @elske7736

title: HARVARD PRE-MED *REALISTIC* DAY-IN-MY-LIFE
body: i worked 3 jobs my first two years of a biomedical engineering undergrad then decided to pick up biochem during my second year even tho i had never done chem before. i was so tired i was seeing spots, couldn’t concentrate and once passed out on a train. best thing i did was cut back and focus on only 1-2 jobs. then take summer and winter subjects so i could perform well in uni without burning out. u don’t have to give everything up but SPACE IT OUT. because you can (and probs will) get physically sick and that WILL push you behind. the people i’ve met who perform the best are smart with their study. if you need to sleep then sleep, because when you’re that tired you won’t remember it anyway and it’s useless work. u might be fine now… but last year undergrad is when that burnout will start to hit HARD.
author: @Zoe-no1sg

title: HARVARD PRE-MED *REALISTIC* DAY-IN-MY-LIFE

title: if MIT were honest  #americancollege #mit #college #collegeadmissions #ivyleague
body:
“weird and quirky, lol, random xD”
actual things at mit:
- clothing optional dorm floors with “exercise poles” installed
- explosives in the dorm courtyard
- annual “virgin sacrifice to the dark lord krotus” themed party
- wrestling in a pit of glitter lube or fake blood with a tesla coil blasting music
- annual piano drop, where a piano is dropped off of the roof
- real clubs include mixology, chocolate science, spinning balls of fire around your body as a performance art, and something called “assassins’ guild”
- decades old milk (the dorm pet)
- multiple dorms where students can paint literally whatever they want — from renditions of renoir to explicit materials to a fully black hallway with red lights
- before my time but a dorm floor once got in trouble for making a giant concrete replica of a bomb to promote a party called “drink ‘til you drop”
- also before my time, over half the lsd on the east coast was once made in an mit dorm
- also also before my time but us copyright law had to be rewritten because of an undergrad’s pet project pirating a ton of books and stuff

measuring bridges in smoots and putting police cars on the roof is honestly kinda tame for us tbh
author: @kimcoleeppling8177

title: if MIT were honest  #americancollege #mit #college #collegeadmissions #ivyleague
body: Funnily enough, if you go to Harvard or M.I.T., you do not go school in Boston.  Both schools are in Cambridge, Massachusetts.  They are on the same subway line.  Boston is across the Charles River from those two schools.  Boston has plenty of highly rated colleges and universities, however.

My favorite response to Harvard people saying they went to school in Boston is enthusiastically ask “oh, did you go to Emerson?”  If they say near Boston , it’s Tufts.  The squirming is delightful.
